title: South Australia Has Too Many Koalas. Scientists Want to Sterilize 22%.
script: While koalas are endangered across most of Australia, one patch in South Australia has the opposite problem — too many. The Mount Lofty Ranges now hold 10 percent of the entire national koala population, and they're eating through the forest faster than it can regrow. Scientists are now calling for mass sterilization of 22 percent of adult females every year. At $34 million over 25 years, it's cheaper than letting them eat themselves to extinction.
